# ClassRegistry

At the intersection of the Registry and Factory patterns lies the
`ClassRegistry`:

- Define global factories that generate new class instances based on
  configurable keys.
- Seamlessly create powerful service registries.
- Integrate with setuptools's `entry_points` system to make your registries
  infinitely extensible by 3rd-party libraries!
- And more!

## Getting Started

Create a registry using the `class_registry.ClassRegistry` class, then
decorate any classes that you wish to register with its `register` method:

```python
from class_registry import ClassRegistry

pokedex = ClassRegistry()

@pokedex.register('fire')
class Charizard(Pokemon):
  ...

@pokedex.register('grass')
class Bulbasaur(Pokemon):
  ...

@pokedex.register('water')
class Squirtle(Pokemon):
  ...
```

To create a class instance from a registry, use the subscript operator:

```python
# Charizard, I choose you!
fighter1 = pokedex['fire']

# CHARIZARD fainted!
# How come my rival always picks the type that my pokémon is weak against??
fighter2 = pokedex['grass']
```

### Advanced Usage

There's a whole lot more you can do with ClassRegistry, including:

- Provide args and kwargs to new class instances.
- Automatically register non-abstract classes.
- Integrate with setuptools's `entry_points` system so that 3rd-party
  libraries can add their own classes to your registries.
- Wrap your registry in an instance cache to create a service registry.
- And more!

For more advanced usage, [check out the documentation on RTD]!

## Requirements

ClassRegistry is compatible with Python versions 3.6, 3.5 and 2.7.

## Installation

Install the latest stable version via pip:

```
pip install class-registry
```

[check out the documentation on rtd]: https://class-registry.readthedocs.org/

---
param attr_name: |-

  corresponding registry key.  This makes it easier to
  perform reverse lookups later.

  Note: if a class already defines this attribute, the
  registry will overwrite it!
param group: |-
  The name of the entry point group that will be used to load

methods.name: class_registry.entry_points.EntryPointClassRegistry.refresh
documentation:
Purges the local cache.
The next access attempt will reload all entry points.

This is useful if you load a distribution at runtime.
Otherwise, it probably serves no useful purpose.

methods.name: class_registry.entry_points.EntryPointClassRegistry._get_cache
documentation: Populates the cache (if necessary) and returns it.
A class registry that loads classes using setuptools entry points.

modules.name: class_registry.auto_register
documentation: coding=utf-8

functions.name: class_registry.auto_register.AutoRegister
documentation:
Creates a metaclass that automatically registers all non-abstract
subclasses in the specified registry.

IMPORTANT:  Python defines abstract as "having at least one
unimplemented abstract method"; specifying {py:class}`ABCMeta` as
the metaclass is not enough!

Example:

```
commands = ClassRegistry(attr_name='command_name')

# Specify ``AutoRegister`` as the metaclass:
class BaseCommand(with_metaclass(AutoRegister(commands))):
  @abstractmethod
  def print(self):
    raise NotImplementedError()

class PrintCommand(BaseCommand):
  command_name = 'print'

  def print(self):
    ...

print(list(commands.items())) # [('print', PrintCommand)]
```

```
param registry

The registry that new classes will be added to.

Note: the registry's attr_name attribute must be set!

param base_type

The base type of the metaclass returned by this function.

99.99% of the time, this should be .
```

modules.name: class_registry.patcher
documentation: coding=utf-8

classes.name: class_registry.patcher.RegistryPatcher

methods.name: class_registry.patcher.RegistryPatcher.__init__
documentation:
---
param args: |-
  Classes to add to the registry.

  This behaves the same as decorating each class with
  `@registry.register`.

  Note: `registry.attr_name` must be set!
param kwargs: |-
  Same as `args`, except you explicitly specify the
  registry keys.

  In the event of a conflict, values in `args` override
  values in `kwargs`.
param registry: A {py:class}`MutableRegistry` instance to patch.
---

documentation: noinspection PyUnusedLocal

documentation: Applies the new values.

documentation: Restores previous settings.

inner_classes.name: class_registry.patcher.RegistryPatcher.DoesNotExist
documentation: Used to identify a value that did not exist before we started.

Creates a context in which classes are temporarily registered with
a class registry, then removed when the context exits.

Note: only mutable registries can be patched!

---
param args: |-

methods.name: class_registry.cache.ClassRegistryInstanceCache.__getitem__
documentation: Returns the cached instance associated with the specified key.

methods.name: class_registry.cache.ClassRegistryInstanceCache.__iter__
documentation:
Returns a generator for iterating over cached instances, using
the wrapped registry to determine sort order.

If a key has not been accessed yet, it will not be included.

methods.name: class_registry.cache.ClassRegistryInstanceCache.warm_cache
documentation:
Warms up the cache, ensuring that an instance is created for
every key in the registry.

Note that this method does not account for any classes that may
be added to the wrapped ClassRegistry in the future.

methods.name: class_registry.cache.ClassRegistryInstanceCache.get_instance_key
documentation:
Generates a key that can be used to store/lookup values in the
instance cache.

```
param key

Value provided to .
```

methods.name: class_registry.cache.ClassRegistryInstanceCache.get_class_key
documentation:
Generates a key that can be used to store/lookup values in the
wrapped {py:class}`ClassRegistry` instance.

This method is only invoked in the event of a cache miss.

```
param key

Value provided to .
```

Wraps a ClassRegistry instance, caching instances as they are
created.

This allows you to create [multiple] registries that cache
INSTANCES locally (so that they can be scoped and garbage-
collected), while keeping the CLASS registry separate.

Note that the internal class registry is copied by reference, so
any classes that are registered afterward are accessible to
both the ClassRegistry and the ClassRegistryInstanceCache.

classes.name: class_registry.registry.RegistryKeyError
documentation:
Used to differentiate a registry lookup from a standard KeyError.

This is especially useful when a registry class expects to extract
values from dicts to generate keys.

Creates a new instance of the class matching the specified key.

```

The corresponding load key.

param args

Positional arguments passed to class initializer.
Ignored if the class registry was initialized with a null
template function.

param kwargs

Keyword arguments passed to class initializer.
Ignored if the class registry was initialized with a null
template function.
```

References:

methods.name: class_registry.registry.BaseRegistry.gen_lookup_key
documentation:
Used by {py:meth}`get` to generate a lookup key.

You may override this method in a subclass, for example if you
need to support legacy aliases, etc.

methods.name: class_registry.registry.BaseRegistry.create_instance
documentation:
Prepares the return value for {py:meth}`get`.

You may override this method in a subclass, if you want to
customize the way new instances are created.

```
param class

The requested class.

param args

Positional keywords passed to .

param kwargs

Keyword arguments passed to .
```

methods.name: class_registry.registry.BaseRegistry.items
documentation:
Iterates over registered classes and their corresponding keys,
in the order that they were registered.

Note: For compatibility with Python 3, this method should
return a generator.

methods.name: class_registry.registry.BaseRegistry.keys
documentation:
Returns a generator for iterating over registry keys, in the
order that they were registered.

Note: For compatibility with Python 3, this method should
return a generator.

methods.name: class_registry.registry.BaseRegistry.values
documentation:
Returns a generator for iterating over registered classes, in
the order that they were registered.

Note: For compatibility with Python 3, this method should
return a generator.

methods.name: class_registry.registry.MutableRegistry.__delitem__

methods.name: class_registry.registry.MutableRegistry.__setitem__
documentation: Provides alternate syntax for registering a class.

methods.name: class_registry.registry.MutableRegistry.register
documentation:
Decorator that registers a class with the registry.

Example:

```
registry = ClassRegistry(attr_name='widget_type')

@registry.register
class CustomWidget(BaseWidget):
  widget_type = 'custom'
  ...

# Override the registry key:
@registry.register('premium')
class AdvancedWidget(BaseWidget):
  ...
```

```
param key

The registry key to use for the registered class.
Optional if the registry's  is set.
```

methods.name: class_registry.registry.MutableRegistry.unregister
documentation:
Unregisters the class with the specified key.

```
param key

The registry key to remove (not the registered class!).

return

The class that was unregistered.

raise

 if the key is not registered.
```

methods.name: class_registry.registry.MutableRegistry._register
documentation: Registers a class with the registry.

methods.name: class_registry.registry.MutableRegistry._unregister
documentation: Unregisters the class at the specified key.

Extends {py:class}`BaseRegistry` with methods that can be used to
modify the registered classes.

methods.name: class_registry.registry.ClassRegistry.__init__
documentation:
---
param attr_name: |-
  If provided, {py:meth}`register` will automatically detect
  the key to use when registering new classes.
param unique: |-
  Determines what happens when two classes are registered with
  the same key:

  - `True`: The second class will replace the first one.
  - `False`: A `ValueError` will be raised.
---

classes.name: class_registry.registry.SortedClassRegistry

methods.name: class_registry.registry.SortedClassRegistry.__init__
documentation:
---
param attr_name: |-
  If provided, {py:meth}`register` will automatically detect
  the key to use when registering new classes.
param reverse: Whether to reverse the sort ordering.
param sort_key: |-
  Attribute name or callable, used to determine the sort value.

  If callable, must accept two tuples of (key, class).
param unique: |-
  Determines what happens when two classes are registered with
  the same key:

  - `True`: The second class will replace the first one.
  - `False`: A `ValueError` will be raised.
---

methods.name: class_registry.registry.SortedClassRegistry.create_sorter
documentation:
Given a sort key, creates a function that can be used to sort
items when iterating over the registry.

A ClassRegistry that uses a function to determine sort order when
iterating.
